title: Wren and the Missing Star
synopsis: When little Wren notices her favorite bedtime star is missing from the sky, she feels sad and worried. With help from her grandmother and a friendly owl, Wren discovers the star is just hiding behind clouds and learns that sometimes the things we love are still there, even when we can't see them.

pages.text: Wren loved looking at the stars before bedtime. Every night, she would find her special star - the brightest one that twinkled just for her. But tonight, something was wrong.

illustration.description: A young South Asian girl with long dark braids stands at a bedroom window, pressing her hands against the glass. She wears purple pajamas with moon patterns. Outside the window, a dark sky shows several stars but one obvious empty spot. Her expression shows confusion and concern. The room has warm yellow lighting from a bedside lamp.
illustration.alt: Wren looking out her bedroom window at the night sky, noticing her star is missing

pages.text: Wren felt sad. Where was her star? She looked and looked, but it wasn't there. 'Grandma Nani!' she called. 'My star is gone!' Tears filled her brown eyes.

illustration.description: Wren sits on her bed looking sad, with small tears on her cheeks. Her grandmother, an elderly woman with silver hair in a colorful sari, sits beside her with a gentle, caring expression. The bedroom has soft purple and yellow colors, with star-patterned bedsheets. A stuffed elephant sits nearby.
illustration.alt: Wren crying on her bed while her grandmother comforts her

pages.text: Grandma Nani hugged Wren tight. 'Don't worry, beta. Let's go find your star together.' Wren felt a little scared to go outside at night, but she wanted to be brave.

illustration.description: Wren and her grandmother stand at the front door, both wearing cozy robes and slippers. Wren holds her grandmother's hand, looking nervous but determined. The doorway opens to a peaceful nighttime garden with purple flowers and a stone path. Warm light spills from the house behind them.
illustration.alt: Wren and Grandma Nani preparing to go outside to look for the missing star

pages.text: In the garden, they met Oliver the owl. 'Hoo-hoo! Why are you awake, little one?' he asked kindly. Wren explained about her missing star. Oliver's big eyes sparkled with wisdom.

illustration.description: A friendly brown owl with large amber eyes perches on a low tree branch, looking down at Wren and her grandmother. The garden setting has purple flowers, green bushes, and a cobblestone path. Wren points up at the sky while explaining. The owl has a gentle, understanding expression.
illustration.alt: Wren and Grandma Nani talking to Oliver the wise owl in the garden

pages.text: 'Sometimes stars hide behind clouds,' Oliver said softly. 'Just because we can't see them doesn't mean they're gone.' He pointed up with his wing. 'Look carefully, brave Wren.'

illustration.description: All three characters look up at the night sky together. Oliver the owl spreads one wing pointing upward. Dark gray clouds drift across parts of the sky, and one bright star peeks through a gap in the clouds. Wren's expression changes from worry to hope and excitement.
illustration.alt: Oliver the owl showing Wren and her grandmother how to look for stars behind clouds

pages.text: Wren looked up and waited. Slowly, the clouds moved away, and there it was - her special star! It twinkled brighter than ever. Wren felt so happy and excited.

illustration.description: Wren jumps with joy, arms raised high, as her bright star shines clearly in the now-clear patch of sky. Her grandmother claps happily beside her, and Oliver the owl hoots with delight from his branch. The star is extra bright and sparkly, dominating the night sky above them.
illustration.alt: Wren celebrating joyfully as her special star reappears from behind the clouds

pages.text: 'Thank you, Oliver!' Wren said gratefully. 'I was scared my star was gone forever, but now I know it was just hiding.' She gave the wise owl a gentle hug.

illustration.description: Wren carefully hugs Oliver the owl while he sits on a low branch. Both have happy, content expressions. Grandma Nani watches with a warm smile. The garden around them glows softly in the starlight, with the special star twinkling brightly overhead.
illustration.alt: Wren hugging Oliver the owl in gratitude while her star shines above

pages.text: Back in her cozy bed, Wren felt peaceful and sleepy. Her star twinkled through the window, and she knew it would always be there, even when clouds hid it. 'Goodnight, star,' she whispered.

illustration.description: Wren lies peacefully in her bed, smiling contentedly as she looks toward the window. Her special star shines brightly through the glass. Grandma Nani tucks her in with a soft blanket. The room has a warm, sleepy atmosphere with soft lighting and the star's gentle glow.
illustration.alt: Wren going to sleep peacefully, watching her star through the bedroom window

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Wren feel when she couldn't find her star?
questions.explanation: Wren felt sad when she couldn't see her special star in the sky.

questions.question: Who helped Wren look for her star?
questions.explanation: Oliver the owl helped Wren understand where her star had gone.

questions.question: Where was Wren's star hiding?
questions.explanation: The star was hiding behind clouds in the sky.

questions.question: How did Wren feel when she found her star?
questions.explanation: Wren felt happy and excited when she saw her star twinkling again.

pages.text: Wren looked up and waited. Slowly, the clouds moved away, and there it was - her special ____! It twinkled brighter than ever.
Wren felt ____ when she couldn't find her star, but Oliver the ____ helped her look behind the clouds.
